result:
A debouncer delays firing the search until the user stops typing for a short window (e.g. 300ms). Without it, every keystroke triggers a request — so typing "react" fires 5 queries, most of which are wasted and can race each other.

Benefits:
- **Fewer requests** — cuts load on your API/DB and avoids rate limits.
- **No race conditions** — an earlier slow response can't overwrite a later one if only the final query fires.
- **Smoother UI** — no flicker from intermediate result sets.
- **Cheaper** — fewer calls to paid APIs, fewer DB queries.

Trade-off: adds ~200–400ms of perceived latency after the last keystroke. Use throttling instead when you *do* want intermediate updates (e.g. scroll handlers).
